_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 21 drug-class identifiers referenced by interaction_rules.drug_class_rules. Splits into two tiers: 13 USER-SELECTABLE classes shown in onboarding/profile (matches lib/core/constants/schema_ids.dart drugClasses) and 8 RULE-ONLY classes used by interaction rules but not surfaced as profile picks (CYP substrate categories, narrow drug families inferred from other selections). Migrating gives clinician control of the user-facing drug-class copy and brand examples.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for drug-class labels, brand examples, and prescription/OTC indicator. Drives drug-interaction warnings (e.g. anticoagulants + vitamin E high-dose → caution).
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/ingredient_interaction_rules.json `drug_class_rules[].drug_class_id`, scripts/data/clinical_risk_taxonomy.json `drug_classes`. Flutter consumer (user-selectable subset): lib/core/constants/schema_ids.dart `drugClasses` + `drugClassLabels`.

field_contract.id: Stable snake_case identifier shared with the user profile and interaction rules.
field_contract.name: User-facing label shown in onboarding and the profile screen (for user_selectable=true entries only).
field_contract.notes: ≤200-char plain-English description shown in tap-to-learn modal.
field_contract.examples: Common brand or generic medication names users might recognize on labels.
field_contract.rx_status: Prescription / OTC indicator. One of: rx_only | otc | mixed.
field_contract.user_selectable: Whether this class appears in onboarding profile picker. False classes are referenced only by interaction rules (CYP substrates, narrow families).

drug_classes.name: Blood thinners
drug_classes.notes: Reduce blood clotting. Many supplements (vitamin E high-dose, fish oil, ginkgo, garlic, turmeric) further inhibit clotting and increase bleeding risk.

drug_classes.name: Antiplatelet medication
drug_classes.notes: Block platelet aggregation. Combining with bleeding-risk supplements (vitamin E, fish oil, ginkgo) compounds the effect.

drug_classes.name: NSAIDs (Ibuprofen, Aspirin regularly)
drug_classes.notes: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatories. Stack-with-supplement risks include GI bleeding (with fish oil, ginkgo) and reduced kidney function (with high-dose vitamin C).

drug_classes.name: Blood pressure medication
drug_classes.notes: Lower blood pressure. Some supplements (licorice, ephedra, high-dose caffeine) raise BP and counteract treatment; others (potassium, magnesium) compound effect.

drug_classes.name: Diabetes medication
drug_classes.notes: Lower blood sugar. Several supplements (cinnamon, berberine, alpha-lipoic acid, chromium) potentiate the glucose-lowering effect; risk of hypoglycemia.

drug_classes.name: Thyroid medication
drug_classes.notes: Replace or modulate thyroid hormone. Iodine, kelp, soy isoflavones, and biotin can alter levels or assay results — separate by 4 hours from levothyroxine.

drug_classes.name: Sedatives / Sleep medication
drug_classes.notes: Promote sleep or reduce anxiety. Combining with supplemental melatonin, valerian, kava, or 5-HTP compounds CNS depression.

drug_classes.name: Immunosuppressants
drug_classes.notes: Suppress immune response after transplant or for autoimmune disease. Echinacea, astragalus, and other immune-stimulating herbs may counteract effect.

drug_classes.name: Statins / Cholesterol medication
drug_classes.notes: Lower LDL cholesterol via HMG-CoA reductase. Red yeast rice contains natural lovastatin and shouldn't combine; CoQ10 is often co-prescribed.

drug_classes.name: Antidepressants (SSRIs/SNRIs)
drug_classes.notes: Increase serotonin (and sometimes norepinephrine) signaling. Combining with St. John's wort, 5-HTP, SAMe, or tryptophan risks serotonin syndrome.

drug_classes.name: MAOIs
drug_classes.notes: Monoamine oxidase inhibitors. Strict dietary restrictions (avoid tyramine-rich foods + supplements); many supplements are contraindicated or risky.

drug_classes.name: Digoxin / Heart rhythm medication
drug_classes.notes: Affect heart muscle contractility and rhythm. Hawthorn, licorice, and grapefruit-related compounds can alter levels.

drug_classes.name: Anticholinergic medication
drug_classes.notes: Block acetylcholine at muscarinic receptors. Combining with anticholinergic herbs (jimsonweed, belladonna) compounds dry mouth, constipation, confusion.

drug_classes.name: Anti-seizure medication
drug_classes.notes: Used for epilepsy and some pain/mood conditions. Several supplements alter levels via CYP induction or affect seizure threshold.

drug_classes.name: Antiarrhythmic medication
drug_classes.notes: Stabilize heart rhythm. Several have narrow therapeutic windows and interact with grapefruit-related compounds, hawthorn, or motherwort.

drug_classes.name: Calcium channel blockers
drug_classes.notes: Lower blood pressure and treat angina by blocking calcium influx. Grapefruit-related compounds raise levels of dihydropyridines.

drug_classes.name: Thiazide diuretics
drug_classes.notes: Increase urine output and lower blood pressure. Cause potassium and magnesium loss; supplements that further deplete or supplement these need monitoring.

drug_classes.name: Lithium
drug_classes.notes: Mood stabilizer with a narrow therapeutic window. Caffeine, salt intake, NSAIDs, and dehydration affect blood levels.

drug_classes.name: Oral contraceptives
drug_classes.notes: Combination or progestin-only birth control pills. St. John's wort and some CYP-inducing supplements can reduce contraceptive efficacy.

drug_classes.name: CYP2D6-substrate drugs
drug_classes.notes: Drugs metabolized by CYP2D6 enzyme. Several supplements (St. John's wort, goldenseal) inhibit or induce this enzyme and alter drug levels.

drug_classes.name: CYP3A4-substrate drugs
drug_classes.notes: Drugs metabolized by CYP3A4 enzyme. Grapefruit, St. John's wort, and many botanicals interact via this pathway and alter drug levels.
